articles.title: Faster aptamer screening finds synthetic alternatives to antibodies in days instead of months
articles.summary: Aptamers are short DNA or RNA strands that can recognize and bind to a specific target molecule with high precision. Similar to antibodies, they can be used to detect these molecules or modulate their activity. Unlike…

articles.title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
articles.summary: A California-based company will design, build and launch a spacecraft for NASA's Aeolus mission to orbit around Mars, where the probe will be the first to provide daily measurements of the planet's global environment.

articles.title: Black hole winds may be robbing giant galaxies of their future stars

articles.title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
articles.summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is falling out of orbit. Can a commercial company build a spacecraft in nine months to save it?

articles.title: Remarkable fossils rewrite the story of how animals conquered the land
articles.summary: Palaeontologists have found new evidence that the early ancestors of amphibians, reptiles and mammals did not have a larval stage with external gills like modern frogs or salamanders

articles.title: Crane pair dances follow hidden rules, with performances revealing partner-driven timing
articles.summary: Animal pairs engage in mutual signaling by simultaneously performing a diverse repertoire of behaviors. A famous example is the sophisticated dance (mutual displays) performed by bird pairs.

articles.title: Climate change boosts soybean production but worsens bean quality

articles.title: Tropical Storm Arthur
articles.summary: The first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season brought intense rainfall and the threat of flash flooding to the U.S. Gulf Coast.

articles.title: Eight ways to sleep well in hot weather
articles.summary: When temperatures rise, sleep often suffers. Hot nights can make it harder to fall asleep, increase waking during the night and leave people feeling less rested the next day.

articles.title: Are useful and error-free quantum computers only two years away?

articles.title: Technology is changing our perspective on nature – at every scale

articles.title: Belgian Nobel laureate Francois Englert dies aged 93
articles.summary: Belgian scientist Francois Englert, a particle physics specialist who won the Nobel Prize in 2013 for his work on the Higgs boson, has died at 93.

articles.title: Scientists trace high-energy ghost particle to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy
articles.summary: "If confirmed, Shadow Blaster would be the first-ever individual dusty star-forming galaxy directly linked to a high-energy neutrino event."

articles.title: Quantum gravity research links continuous parameters to local operators within the theory itself

articles.title: A quantum state that lasts forever may finally be within our grasp
articles.summary: Defying the laws of thermodynamics, experiments are beginning to show that a quantum state that is frozen forever might not be impossible. If we can tame it, it could unlock whole new types of matter
